what_it_does: A single-file interim CLI that builds and queries a semantic-search index over local Claude-session transcripts. It chunks transcripts by turn, embeds them through a local pplx-embed-context-v1-4b-compatible service, stores L2-normalized INT8 vectors as numpy and metadata as JSONL, then supports top-K cosine-similarity search.
observations: Self-contained interim tool with no framework beyond numpy/urllib/argparse.
Embeds via an OpenAI-compatible local endpoint and explicitly expects 2560-dimensional INT8 unnormalized vectors.
Stores index as vectors.npy plus meta.jsonl under ~/.cache/company/transcript-search.
Parses YAML frontmatter and splits transcripts on '## Turn ' headings to make one chunk per turn.
Uses environment variable PPLX_EMBED_URL with a default localhost endpoint and a /health check.
Implements a 'fail loud' policy: sys.exit or RuntimeError on missing service, wrong dimension, or empty corpus.
The search command reads each hit file into a 'snip' variable but never prints it.
standouts: Explicit handling of INT8 unnormalized vectors by L2-normalizing before cosine dot product.
Strong 'registry-is-truth' / 'fail loud' operational documentation in the module docstring.
Working but labeled throwaway interim, verified by use rather than by tests.
conventions: Docstring maps WIRING, CORPUS, and USAGE sections.
Environment override with hardcoded default for service URL.
Uses os.path.expanduser for corpus and cache paths.
JSONL metadata paired with a numpy array, aligned by row index.
concerns: Search reads the entire matched transcript file into memory via 'snip' but never uses or displays it, so it is dead code and wasted I/O.
Index command always rebuilds from scratch; no incremental or delta indexing.
MAX_CHARS slice may cut text mid-word or mid-sentence before embedding.
No concurrency/locking around writing vectors.npy and meta.jsonl.
No validation that response embedding indices actually match the request order beyond sorting by index.
Hardcoded EXPECT_DIM and paths make the file brittle to model or layout changes.
errors='replace' on file reads can silently corrupt transcript content.
notes: A lean, fail-loud interim semantic-search CLI for local Claude transcripts, backed by a local embedding service and numpy/JSONL storage.
questions: Why does cmd_search read the full file into 'snip' but not print or use a snippet?
Does the index command support incremental updates, or is it always a full rebuild?
How are multi-line YAML frontmatter values handled by the simple split-on-colon parser?
Is the /health endpoint path guaranteed by the embed-pplx service, or is it an assumption?
How are duplicate session files or re-exported sessions deduplicated?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated interim search purpose; the only mismatch is that search appears to prepare a content snippet but never outputs it, suggesting intended snippet display is unimplemented.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a quick but trustworthy semantic retrieval layer for session transcripts while a permanent memory system is built elsewhere, prioritizing correct embedding handling and loud failure over graceful degradation.
novelty: The explicit pairing of INT8 unnormalized 2560-d pplx-embed-context vectors with L2 normalization for cosine search, wrapped in a single throwaway script that documents its service wiring as registry-is-truth.
contribution: A self-contained working prototype that demonstrates transcript chunking, batched embedding, normalized cosine retrieval, and CLI index/search; it is useful as a reference implementation for embedding integration and chunking strategy even if later replaced.
summary_for_embedding: transcript_search.py is a single-file interim CLI that builds a cosine-similarity semantic index of Claude session transcripts using a local pplx-embed-context-v1-4b embedding service and stores vectors and metadata as numpy/JSONL for top-K search.
intention_for_embedding: Intended as a temporary, fail-loud local semantic search tool over exported Claude transcripts until a permanent memory system replaces it.

symbols.does: Verifies the local embedding service is reachable and reports ok via its /health endpoint, exiting loudly otherwise.
symbols.description: Performs a pre-flight health check on the embed-pplx service. It uses urllib to call the HEALTH endpoint and sys.exit with a diagnostic message if the service is unreachable or not ready, embodying the file's fail-loud policy.

symbols.does: Batches texts and POSTs them to the local /v1/embeddings endpoint, returning a float32 array of shape (n, EXPECT_DIM).
symbols.description: Sends input texts in batches to the configured embedding endpoint, requests INT8 quantization, sorts the response rows by index, validates the embedding dimension, and accumulates the results into a single numpy array. It raises on transport or shape errors rather than falling back.

symbols.does: L2-normalizes an array along the last axis, guarding against zero norms.
symbols.description: Computes per-row L2 norms and divides the input by them, replacing zero norms with one to avoid division by zero. This turns unnormalized embeddings into unit vectors so that cosine similarity can be computed as a dot product.

symbols.does: Parses a markdown transcript file into per-turn chunks with associated metadata.
symbols.description: Reads a transcript file, extracts YAML frontmatter fields such as title, project, and session_id, splits the body on '## Turn ' headings, and yields chunks capped at MAX_CHARS along with metadata including path, session_id, project, title, and block index.

symbols.does: Builds or refreshes the vector index over the entire transcript corpus.
symbols.description: Checks the embedding service, discovers all .md files under the corpus directory, chunks them, embeds the chunks in batches, L2-normalizes the vectors, and writes vectors.npy and meta.jsonl to the index directory. It is a full rebuild with no incremental logic.

symbols.does: Runs a top-K cosine-similarity search for a query against the stored index.
symbols.description: Loads the saved vectors and metadata, embeds and normalizes the query, computes cosine similarities as dot products between normalized vectors, prints the top-K metadata including score, project, title, path, and block number, but does not display the matched text content.

symbols.does: Sets up argparse subcommands and dispatches to cmd_index or cmd_search.
symbols.description: Defines the 'index' and 'search' CLI subcommands, parses arguments, and invokes the corresponding handler function. It is the standard entry point for the script.
